product_name: Sainsbury's Strawberry Conserve, Taste the Difference 340g
title: A Berry-Perfect Layer for Trifle Lovers
text: This strawberry preserve was absolutely delicious and worked beautifully in my trifle. The flavor tasted fresh and fruity, with a nice balance of sweetness and real strawberry notes—not artificial at all. The texture was perfect: thick enough to hold its layers without soaking the sponge too much, yet smooth enough to spread easily. It added a bright, fresh burst of flavor that tied the whole dessert together. I’d definitely use this again for baking and desserts, not just toast.

product_name: Sainsbury's Apricot Halves in Fruit Juice 411g
title: A refreshing, fruity treat.
text: The Sainsbury's apricot halves in fruit juice were a very pleasant surprise. The ring pull on the top of the can saved having to use a tin opener, a small but useful consideration. There were 25 small apricot halves in total, which would be an ideal portion each for a family of three or four particularly if served with ice cream or cream as a quick dessert. The first taste seemed to be slightly bitter or tart, but this was probably because I was expecting them to be very sweet, which was not the case. The texture was firm, not slippery and the fruit juice was lovely and also not too sweet. All in all, they were an enjoyable, refreshing and fruity treat.

product_name: Colour Protect Shampoo 250ml
title: TERRIBLE NEW FORMULA! SMELLS AWFUL
text: I have been a loyal user of L’Oréal Elvive Colour Protect Shampoo for years, and I have always loved how it made my hair look and feel. The old formula left my hair glossy, vibrant, and smelling amazing – I received so many compliments on how healthy and beautiful my hair was. However, the recent change in formula has completely ruined the product for me. The new version smells unpleasant, lacks the luxurious feel of the previous formula, and leaves my hair feeling dull and lifeless. I honestly can’t believe how much the product has changed, and it’s incredibly disappointing. After years of using this shampoo as my go-to, I’m sad to say I will no longer be purchasing it. It’s frustrating to see a product that worked so well for so long be changed for the worse. I really hope L’Oréal listens to their customers and brings back the original formula. Until then, I’ll be looking for an alternative – one that actually makes my hair look and smell as good as it used to.

About 2/3 months ago we noticed that the burgers suddenly were chewy and full of gristle after cooking, which we had previously never experienced with this product. At first we thought this was possibly a one off event and have bought the product a few times since but sadly, we have experienced the same problem with the burgers still being chewy and full of gristle - almost inedible. product_name: Sainsbury's Sweetcorn, Super Sweet 1kg
title: I'm never buying tinned sweetcorn again!
text: It's 30 degrees today so I did my version of lukewarm Spanish country salad which has sweetcorn in it along with the usual tinned tuna, boiled potatoes and boiled eggs. I added a handful of this sweetcorn to warm through in the cooking water from the potatoes and boiled eggs, drained it and combined it with raw tomatoes, grated carrot, onion, olives and capers - I love the little Sainsbury's non-pareilles better than olives - plus seasoning and oil and vinegar. It's not authentic but I like it on holiday or when I'm at home and it's too hot to cook. This sweetcorn is almost a third of the price of the top tinned brand and as sweet without annoying husks. No waste, it needed minimal heat in the kitchen (which was good on the hottest day of the year so far) and I saved even more money by using the residual heat from the water!. I could have made it even cheaper with tinned new potatoes but decided to splash out on fresh ones.

product_name: Leap Wild MSC Sockeye Smoked Salmon 100g
title: Small but not ugly
text: Have bought this particular smoked salmon for a couple of months and put up with truncated pieces of salmon - half the size of other wild, smoked salmon - because I mainly use it in salads and pasta dishes. However, last week - after cooking two bunches of lovely Sainsbury's asparagus - I attempted to wrap salmon around each bunch as a ‘showcase lunch’ for two. It looked downright silly with small pieces falling off the side of the asparagus bundles. Admittedly, my partner ate his very happily and so did I, after quietly puzzling the question of size. So, to answer other observers, the taste seemed fine to me but the colour was (and is) deeper than you usually get with smoked salmon. Can only conclude that the lower price relates to leftover salmon from packs of 'normal-sized' salmon. It may, however, be marinaded for longer than the normal pieces. Tenderising? As I prefer to go 'wild' rather than venture into the dangers of Scottish-farmed salmon, I shall probably continue to buy this until the pieces shrink to less than the size of an old Christmas postage stamp. But I won’t attempt to wrap a bunch of asparagus with it again.

product_name: NESQUIK® CHOCOLATE MILKSHAKE POWDER TUB
title: Great for families
text: I really like the Nesquik chocolate powder, it’s been a favourite in our house for years. I love the packaging on this Nesquik tub — it’s great that it shows ideas on how to repurpose it, like turning it into a pen pot, instead of just throwing it away. The tub itself is very big, so it lasts a long time. It tastes nice, although the chocolate flavour isn’t very strong. It’s perfect for me because I can add my own lactose-free milk, as I can’t have much regular milk and can’t have pre-made milkshakes. Sometimes you can’t get every lump out, but you can get most of them. If you leave it to sit for a bit it does start to separate, so it’s best to keep a spoon handy to give it a quick re-stir. Overall, a great product and one I’d happily keep buying — I also love the strawberry flavour.

product_name: NESCAFÉ Original Decaf
title: Surprisingly Rich and Flavourful Decaf Experience
text: As an avid coffee enthusiast who is health-conscious and must steer clear of caffeine, my quest for a decaffeinated coffee that doesn't compromise on flavour has been both challenging and rewarding. Nescafé Decaf, a delightful surprise, has not only met but exceeded my expectations, earning a solid place in my daily routine. Given my restrictions, I approached Nescafé Decaf with a mix of scepticism and anticipation. The aroma that wafted from the jar upon opening the seal immediately intrigued me. The coffee looked and smelled no different from their caffeinated counterparts, a testament to the care taken in preserving the essence of the beans. Nescafé Decaf's rich and robust character has defied the stereotype of lacklustre decaf coffees. Its remarkable taste and the absence of bitterness make it a standout choice for caffeine-conscious individuals who refuse to compromise on flavour.

product_name: Kellogg's All-Bran Breakfast Cereal 750g
title: Appalling new recipe All Bran
text: I have been eating Kellogg's All Bran Original for breakfast for 67 years and was appalled to discover that Kellogg's had changed the recipe without any forewarning. Like everyone else here, I can't stand the new Fibre Plus version as it tastes and looks nothing like the original. To make matters worse there's less fibre in the new recipe. As with other reviewers, I kept receiving Fibre Plus from Sainsbury's, even though I'd ordered All Bran Original, and returned the box unopened every time. After several weeks of this happening, and unaware at that time of the recipe change, I emailed Sainsbury's to advise them that there appeared to be an issue with their website, which was, and still is, showing the product as All Bran Original. They told me they would investigate and correct the error. Obviously, that hasn't happened. I checked the other major supermarket sites and discovered that their customers were also returning boxes of the Fibre Plus because they didn't like the recipe. I also contacted Kellogg's directly, through their website, and asked why I could no longer obtain All Bran Original. They replied that, after extensive research, and conducting focus groups, they felt that the new recipe was a big improvement on the original because it no longer produced a pile of dust in the bottom of the wrapping, and it was cheaper to produce. I'm hoping that, with so many of us returning the Fibre Plus, Kellogg's will get the message that they've made a huge mistake and reinstate the Original recipe. I would advise other devotees to contact Kellogg's direct.

product_name: NESQUIK® CHOCOLATE MILKSHAKE POWDER TUB
title: Disappointing
text: I received this product free of charge from Influenster in exchange for my honest review. I absolutely love chocolate milkshake so I was looking forward to trying this Nesquik one with my kids. It comes as a powder in a resealable tub and I made up the milkshake with milk according to the instructions on the packaging. It formed a chocolatey brown colour but it didn’t thicken at all. It was so thin and watery and despite using a whisk, still was a bit grainy. It smelled of chocolate but the flavour was lacking. It was far too sweet and really just tasted like sugary milk. Even my kids thought it was too sweet. Overall very disappointing.

product_name: Sainsbury's 30 Days Matured British Beef Picanha Steak, Taste the Difference 225g
title: Dire. Sinewy and inedible
text: I have only tried this cut in a restaurant and really enjoyed it. Bought it on Monday, eating (well, trying to) on Wednesday. Utterly unacceptable quality, even at half the (full) price paid. It looks (on both sides) to be a lean steak. But it is just flesh irrevocably melded onto inedible sinew. Chew for a minute and you’ll still not have made any in-roads. I’ve ended up with a half-untouched steak and several discarded, chewed lumps on the side of my plate. I rarely get annoyed enough to return items but there is going to be a rumpus if I don’t get my money back. I think (haven’t got my receipt to hand) that a single steak was in the region of £7 for one. Frankly, 50 pence would be fifty pence too much. Utterly vile and disappointing.

The pizza went in the bin and we went to bed hungry.

product_name: Leap Wild MSC Sockeye Smoked Salmon 100g
title: Interesting, tasty flavour
text: I love smoked salmon and consume it regularly, but until now had not tried wild smoked salmon. This one was interesting in that the texture felt much firmer and the taste much more distinctive than my usual mild smoked (farmed) salmon. It is sliced quite thickly. I had it with my favourite mustard and dill sauce on seeded sourdough toast, for brunch, and it made a delicious and substantial meal. I think it being priced at more than twice the cost of my usual buy would preclude me from making this a regular purchase, but as an occasional treat I would definitely enjoy it again.

text: I drink this coffee most mornings and it makes a good, drinkable cup. I have to admit that I'm not anyone's idea of a coffee expert but then this product isn't aimed at the connoisseurs, it's aimed at people like me who want to drink something hot and full of caffeine in the morning! I usually use one teaspoon per mug for a medium-strength taste, but two teaspoons gives you a stronger taste if that's your thing. It's a large jar so usually lasts me a couple of months or so, providing very good value. It comes in a glass jar for recycling and the usual foil lid to keep it fresh.

title: Love Biotiful!
text: Been purchasing Biotiful's products for a good couple of years now and am always excited to see that they've released a new range and/or flavour (Well, maybe not always. I'm not exactly crazy about mango-flavoured foods in general, though I like the actual fruit itself.) - even better ones with an emphasis on protein! Anyway, this new strawberry-flavoured kefir drink is, rather unsurprisingly to me, pretty nice. Enjoyable and good for you, I can only recommend it, really

product_name: Sainsbury's Butter Croissants, Taste the Difference x4
title: Not Up to Taste the Difference Standards
text: These butter croissants were a real let‑down, especially compared with the usual quality of the Taste the Difference range. Like the last couple of disappointed reviewers, the issues are the same. Some croissants feel flat and doughy, others overly dry, and none have that proper flaky lift or buttery aroma you’d expect at this price point. They look premium, but the eating experience just doesn’t match. From a health perspective, croissants are naturally high in saturated fat and refined carbs, so when the flavour and texture don’t deliver, it makes the indulgence feel even less worthwhile. For a treat item, you want it to feel special — and these simply don’t. A rare miss for the range and not something I’d rush to buy again.

product_name: Sainsbury's Meatball Pasta Bake Ready Meal For 4 1.5kg
title: Oh Dear
text: My darling wife bought this "masterpiece" as a quick option for dinner. Not expecting a gourmet meal my expectation was that it would be a hearty meal for the family requiring little effort to prepare. Oh how wrong I was. The cooking instructions are clear, and easy or anyone to follow. The smell coming from the oven while it cooked was appetising and promised a filling and tasty meal. How wrong I was. On removing from the oven it looked like any other pre-packaged meatball bake. With the smell of tomato, herbs and a slight aroma of cooked cheese. Slicing it into serving we found there was plenty to go around between the 3 of us in the house, and because he's a little baby to my darling wife he the dog was given a serving. This was when the disappointment started. The sauce is sweet enough that I suspect they emptied a bag of sugar in the mix, the pasta was rubbery (even my poor cooking skills can't usually manage that). The cheese despite looking good had the consistency of jam, and tasted like old boots smell. The meatballs however take the winner medal. The consistency was 'interesting', the only thing I can compare it to is a particularly hefty poonami from when I still had to change baby nappies. Somehow the meat seemed to taste of nothing, yet awful at the same time. In the hope save something from the meal we opted to try just the pasta and sauce, but couldn't quite manage that. Even our Romanian Rescue dog who has lived on the streets scavenging for food in an interesting new take on his usual behaviour opted to not eat rather than have his share. On the plus side, Sainsbury's Cream of Tomato soup is a handy backup to any meal.

product_name: NESCAFÉ Iced Coffee Latte
title: Tastes lovely
text: Nescafé Iced Coffee Latte is a smooth, refreshing option for coffee lovers looking for a quick caffeine fix on the go. It is a nice balance between the rich flavor of coffee and the creamy sweetness of a latte. The taste is mellow, with no bitterness, making it perfect for those who prefer a lighter, milkier coffee. The convenience of having it pre-made and ready to drink straight from the carton is a big plus, especially on hot days or during a busy schedule. It’s not overly sweet, which is great. Overall, Nescafé Iced Coffee Latte is a satisfying, easy-to-drink option that delivers a pleasant blend of flavor, convenience, and refreshment. Ideal for casual coffee drinkers or anyone in need of a cool, caffeinated treat.

product_name: Coca-Cola 1.25L Zero Sugar Bottle
title: Needs no introduction
text: A short spell of hot weather spurred me on to buy some Coke, but for a change I thought I’d try the Zero for the first time. Tried it just with ice & lemon & also with rum and a chunk of fresh lime in a Cuba Libre. My verdict is that it’s a bit disappointing compared to Coke original, both on its own and as a mixer. There was an artificial over sweet taste to it which I didn’t like much. It seems unbalanced & a bit clumsy. I won’t be buying it again, even though it is half the price of Coke original.

text: So sad this coffee has changed in flavour and consistency since the label change. I’ve Frank’s it for over 15 years and it’s been a huge family favourite all that time. However, the last 6 months it no longer has a lovely crema and instead bubbles and then disappears into a thin standard coffee. I bough some of the old label jars from B&m and HOMEBARGAINS and both were amazing and tasted like the old stuff but every jar of the new stuff just doesn’t hit like it used to. We recently tried the Nescafé smooth crema and that doesn’t have the flavour of the espresso but has the right consistency of the crema. Please don’t tell us it hasn’t changed as we all know we are the consumers and we are telling you it is not the same. Please bring back the original coffee as this one is not good at all.

product_name: Sainsbury's Simply Seasoned With Salt & Pepper Cooked British Roast Beef Top Sliced, Taste the Difference x5 110g
title: The beef lottery...
text: I've written a review on this beef in the past, because there was a period when it was consistently riddled with fat (more fat than actual beef, in fact), or it was sliced as thick as door stops and/or swimming in slime and smelling like a corpse. I don't know if Sainsburys had changed their supplier at that time as it was previously always very good. But, some months later the quality and taste improved for a while and was good (though not superb like it had been previously). However, before I got to amending my review the quality dropped again and has remained that way ever since. It isn't as 'consistently bad' as it was during that prior period, but is so on a somewhat regular basis. So, unfortunately buying this beef has been a bit of a lottery for a number of years now. And, if I'd had the time or inclination to request a refund for every pack I've had to throw away over the years I could probably purchase a whole cow with the refunded money. It's a shame, because when you do get a good pack it is half decent. Again, not superb any more like it used to be in terms of quality AND taste/flavor... but decent - When not riddled with fat, sliced into just two or three slices the thickness of boot leather, smelling like a corpse, drowning in slime or a combination of all or any of the above. Still, as stated I've had to bin multiple packs over the years which were too ghastly to eat. Especially so more recently including the last two orders in a row. And the regularity of having to do so has gradually increased to a point where I'm delivered meat to feed the dustbin more often than meat to feed myself. Therefore, I'm no longer willing to risk playing the 'Sainsburys Beef Lottery' myself so can not in good conscience recommend you buy it.

product_name: Sainsbury's Giraffe Bloomer Bread 800g
title: Oh dear Sainsbury's - your bread is awful
text: So Sainsbury's, you fire all your trained bakers to save money, using normal staff to bake the bread and then expect your bread to stay the same when brought in from outside the store? Well let me help you out here, bringing unbaked dough in that has apparently been proved then frozen does not produce very nice bread at all. The crust is now well, very hard and if you are lucky, it stays on the loaf or as in my photo here of the 400g version shows, it just crumbles off. Your bread used to be the nicest out of the supermarkets, now its worse even than Asda and Tesco. Buy this if you have to folks but don't go out of your way - Morrisons and Asda extra special baked in store bread is so much better.

product_name: Pizza Express Pre-Rolled Pizza Dough 400g
title: Nothing like Pizza Express
text: When I saw this on the shelf, I was excited to try it due to the pizza express name. The box came with clear instructions. It was very difficult to unroll the dough, maybe it needed to be allowed to warm up slightly in the same way as ready roll pastry. I have seen other people in their reviews say that the dough was difficult to remove from the paper however the instructions were very clear that it should be left on the paper for cooking. I guess this means it would not be suitable for a pizza oven, but I was doing it in my kitchen oven so not a problem. The dough made a large rectangular pizza and I put my toppings quite close to the edge. After the minimum required time in the oven, the edges were golden brown and quite crisp. I did not want my pizza to be hard, so I took it out of the oven at this point. After cooking, it was very easy to remove from the backing paper with no sticking at all. Unfortunately, the underneath of the pizza base was still completely white and soft, although I would say that it was cooked through. Upon eating it was average at best, very disappointing as I was expecting it to be similar to a Pizza Express restaurant pizza and it certainly wasn’t. I had half leftover, but the box was clear in the instructions that it should not be reheated and as we don’t like cold pizza it was not used. I would definitely not buy again

title: Virtually Tasteless Sausage Meat & Over-Boiled Egg
text: The title says it all, really. How this can be considered a "Taste The Difference" range is beyond me. The meat is improperly seasoned, the egg rubber, dry and hard, the breadcrumb coating not at all crispy, rather it's actually quite sloppy ... and it tastes of virtually nothing. Of late, it's almost as if Sainsbury's add this moniker to anything and everything and subsequently increase the selling price. Wine, ham, scotch eggs ... anything at all, really! Anyways, if you want to taste any difference, do what I will now continue to do: buy from a local butcher or deli instead, where the product is made either the same day or the day before. Just a thought...

product_name: Neutrogena T/GEL Anti-Dandruff Sensitive Scalp Shampoo 150ml
title: A Clean and Healthier Feel to my Hair
text: I never thought about trying a product like this before as I've tended to stick to my usual shampoo and conditioner without really thinking about my scalp. However, as someone who uses dry shampoo between washes, I knew I needed something to give my hair and scalp a bit of TLC. This product fits the bill. It has a nice texture, it's a clear gel and it's fragrance free, which I think is a good thing and a welcome change from the medicated smell of similar shampoos. This made my hair and scalp feel clean and fresh and I have noticed a reduction in irritation. Overall I recommend this product and would purchase again.

product_name: McVitie's Fig Rolls Biscuits 200g
title: Highly recommend
text: Fig Roll Biscuits have long been a staple in the world of snacks, and for good reason. The combination of sweet fig paste encased in a soft, chewy biscuit offers a delightful contrast of textures and flavours. Each bite delivers a rich sweetness from the figs, complemented by the slightly buttery and crumbly outer biscuit. Very fresh Taste and Texture: The fig filling is lusciously sweet with a hint of natural tanginess, which balances beautifully with the mild flavour of the biscuit. This makes them a perfect snack to pair with tea or coffee. The biscuit shell is not overly sweet, allowing the fig filling to really shine without overwhelming the taste buds. Packaging and Presentation: Fig Roll Biscuits are typically packaged in an easy-to-open sleeve that keeps them fresh and makes them a convenient snack for on-the-go. The presentation is simple, yet the uniformity and golden hue of the biscuits are visually appealing. Nutritional Value: While they are undeniably tasty, it should be noted that Fig Roll Biscuits are relatively high in sugar and calories, which is something to keep in mind for those watching their intake. However, the figs do offer some dietary fibre and essential minerals, making them a slightly healthier option than some other sugary snacks. Overall Impression: Fig Roll Biscuits are a classic treat that evoke a sense of nostalgia for many, and they continue to hold their own in the competitive snack market. With their delightful taste and comforting texture, they remain a favourite for anyone looking for a sweet yet satisfying snack. Whether enjoyed with a hot beverage or simply on their own, Fig Roll Biscuits are sure to please those who appreciate the unique taste of figs.￼
full_text: Highly recommend. Fig Roll Biscuits have long been a staple in the world of snacks, and for good reason. The combination of sweet fig paste encased in a soft, chewy biscuit offers a delightful contrast of textures and flavours. Each bite delivers a rich sweetness from the figs, complemented by the slightly buttery and crumbly outer biscuit. Very fresh Taste and Texture: The fig filling is lusciously sweet with a hint of natural tanginess, which balances beautifully with the mild flavour of the biscuit. This makes them a perfect snack to pair with tea or coffee. The biscuit shell is not overly sweet, allowing the fig filling to really shine without overwhelming the taste buds. Packaging and Presentation: Fig Roll Biscuits are typically packaged in an easy-to-open sleeve that keeps them fresh and makes them a convenient snack for on-the-go. The presentation is simple, yet the uniformity and golden hue of the biscuits are visually appealing. Nutritional Value: While they are undeniably tasty, it should be noted that Fig Roll Biscuits are relatively high in sugar and calories, which is something to keep in mind for those watching their intake. However, the figs do offer some dietary fibre and essential minerals, making them a slightly healthier option than some other sugary snacks. Overall Impression: Fig Roll Biscuits are a classic treat that evoke a sense of nostalgia for many, and they continue to hold their own in the competitive snack market. With their delightful taste and comforting texture, they remain a favourite for anyone looking for a sweet yet satisfying snack. Whether enjoyed with a hot beverage or simply on their own, Fig Roll Biscuits are sure to please those who appreciate the unique taste of figs.￼

product_name: Sainsbury's Giraffe Bloomer Bread 400g
title: Armour plated bread
text: I don't think the name giraffe bread suits this, it should be called rhino bread. I had my delivery including this loaf at 10:30am, at 11am I went to make a sandwich, a simple task you might think. not so. The loaf wasn't dark or overbaked so looked fine, once unwrapped I soon discovered that it was rock solid, there was no give even in the centre of the loaf, I tried to cut it with my usual bread knife without any joy, the blade just kept skating over the impenetrable crust, even after turning it sideways, I found my very sharp Global Chef's Bread Knife and set to again... The crust gave way this time but shattered all over the worktop, literally depositing fragments everywhere far from the chopping board, after eventually managing to get two slices form the loaf I made my sandwich, then discovered that even though I have all my own teeth it was hard labour trying to chew the crust, after a couple of minutes I gave up with an aching jaw. What I eventually did was saw all of the crust off the loaf and use the centre to make a small batch of croutons for future use, I thought about trying to turn the whole loaf into breadcrumbs, but didn't want to damage my blender. Definitely a product to avoid.

product_name: NESCAFÉ Caramel Latte
title: Overly sweet
text: was really looking forward to trying the Nescafé Caramel Latte sachets, but honestly, I could barely finish a cup. The sweetness hits you like a sugar bomb — it’s more like drinking melted caramel candy than coffee. Even after adding extra hot water to dilute it, it still tasted syrupy and artificial. To put it in perspective, I usually take one sugar in my regular coffee, but this sachet tasted like someone had already dumped in five heaping teaspoons of sugar before sealing it. The caramel flavor completely overpowers any coffee taste, leaving behind a sticky aftertaste that lingers far too long. If you like your coffee to actually taste like coffee, this isn’t for you. Unless you have a serious sweet tooth, I’d recommend skipping this and making your own caramel latte at home — you’ll save your taste buds (and probably your teeth).

title: Smells amazing
text: I really love the scent of this detergent. It smells floral but sweet at the same time. It washed my clothes really well and leaves then smelling amazing and they definitely have that soft clean feeling to them. It says on the back of the bottle that after multiple washes it's removes bobbling which I think is an amazing idea altho I'm yet to tell any difference if I'm honest. Your ment to use the lid as a measure and I'm gonna be honest it did confuse me slightly, I just think maybe they should make it abit simpler and have a measure that does a small load and a large load. So the lid is 40ml, for a small loads its 35ml and for the large you 2x 25ml . I was stuck for a bit trying to work out how I was gonna measure that and then noticed in side the lid there are actual number measures. I didn't actually see them until the sun was on them as they are hardly noticeable. My eye sight ain't bad so for some one that doesn't have bad eyes they would totally miss this. I think the product is good and it does its job, it's cleans and smells amazing but the lid in my opinion needs improvement or the measurements needs to be simplified.

product_name: Harbour Salmon Co. Smoked Salmon Gravadlax Style 100g
title: An attempt at a classic, which failed
text: I have been fortunate in the past to enjoy high end salmon prepared in this way. Whilst I do not expect for the price to have the same quality as that prepared in The Queens Grills on the Cunard Line I do expect it to taste good and for the curing to have been better than mine was. Unlike other reviews, I did not enjoy this product. The texture of the salmon was coarse, harsh and over salted, which was probably why it was dry in the eating. Additionally, the slices were not thin, thick lumps as if a novice was practising slicing as only only one small piece could be described as being thin enough. I will not be giving this another chance as I suspect the problems I experienced were down to the quantities of stuff used to cure the salmon.

product_name: Jam Shed Shiraz 750ml
title: Fruity but too sweet
text: Fruity but sweet I found this shiraz wine to be very easy drinking, in fact too easy as to me it didn't taste as though I were drinking wine at all as it was very sweet with no dry tanin finish. Shiraz is usually one of my red wines of choice but this wine was totally different and unlike any Shiraz I had tried before. It was an attractive deep red colour in the glass and full of fruit flavours but it had no dry finish at all so it felt more like drinking a fruit drink instead of wine. I tried it both at room temperature with a roast dinner and then chilled as suggested on the bottle and chilling definitely suited the style of wine more and reminded me of sangria. I can imagine it would go down well chilled outside at a party or with friends, especially those who were not usually drinkers of red wine. I enjoyed trying this wine and while it was pleasant unfortunately, it was too sweet for me and did not have the wine taste that I prefer so I would not buy it.

product_name: Repairing Papaya Hair Food Shampoo For Damaged Hair 350ml
title: Good but mightn’t work for your curly hair!
text: Good but unfortunately not a product I’d get again - it works and there’s nothing wrong with it, it simply doesn’t agree with my curly / wavy hair (I’ll definitely finish the bottle though!). This shampoo cleans the hair well, I don’t need to go back in and do a second wash, one is enough; but my hair does not feel totally dry and stripped either, the balance between cleanliness and moisture is good. However I do find that something in the formulation does not agree with my curly / wavy hair - after washing with this I really struggled to get a natural curl in my hair, even though I followed my usual styling routine. I have used this shampoo again though, as when I’m in a hurry and don’t have time for a full curl routine, this leaves my hair feeling clean and fresh but not weighed down and lank. I loved that the bottle had a seal for hygiene and safety; it’s quite a rarity but I think it’s a superb idea! The packaging style is appealing and consistent across the product range. Aesthetically I would’ve preferred a glossy bottle, but I think the semi matte finish works and also improves grip on the bottle when wet.

product_name: Erdinger Weissbier Wheat Beer Bottle 500ml
title: Bland, over-hyped and over-marketed weissbier
text: I've been drinking Weissbiers for over forty years and Erdinger is the most heavily marketed Weissbier on the planet. They even give away free tastings at the Heineken Brewery in return for reviews to ramp up their ratings. There's something fundamentally underhand about that in my opinion. As far as Wheat/Weissbiers go, this is just about the most bland and watery and tasteless, meanest example of the German brewers art. In my opinion it's one of the worst biers in Germany. It's the absolute last on my list of preferred Weissbiers. Sainsburys are one of the few shops in the UK that stock Weihenstephaner so why anyone would buy this rubbish when Weihenstephaner is available for a few pence more is beyond me. Also substantially better than this is Franziskaner and Schöfferhofer but Paulaner too. In fact just about every alternative Weissbier to Erdinger is better than this, even the American newcomer to the genre, Blue Moon. Don't just read my negative comments and dismiss them! Do try the alternatives for yourself. You don't need to be a bier connoisseur to taste the difference. Don't be missing out on the good stuff!

product_name: Eat Natural PRO
title: Difficult to chew and little taste
text: I have been given the opportunity to try the Eat Natural Protein Bars which came in a multipack of 3 bars. The bars contain all natural ingredients and provide a good amount of energy. This particular flavour is a combination of peanut, dark chocolate and protein-rich soya. It is a good source of protein and a good source of fibre and it is also Gluten Free. After taking the first bite I have found that the protein is quite difficult to bite into and is hard in texture. It is not very easy to chew through the bar into smaller pieces and the taste is similar. I found that the protein bar tastes quite bland. It lacks real flavour and I cannot taste the peanut and chocolate combination that should be there in taste. The bar is more of an appetiser rather than a healthy snack due to the small rectangular shaped bar that is on offer. The bar is wrapper in clear packaging in which you can clearly see through and see the contents. It is made from a thin film of plastic which is not recyclable. The outer packaging which contains 3 rectangular protein bars is made from cardboard packaging which can be recycled which is great for the environment.

product_name: Sainsbury's Lime & Chilli Chicken Breast Chunks 80g
title: Lunch- delicious, quick, painless!
text: Just bought one of these to try, now wishing I’d bought more! Keenly priced, it’s a small pack, but I got 2 rolls from it. The chicken chunks are tender and very very tasty; the fresh intensity of the intense lime and spice mix works well with sweetish brioche-type bread. It would also form the basis of a lovely salad, or make a great pancake filling. A word about the chilli: I don’t do chilli just for the sake of it and would never sacrifice flavour for heat; this chicken gets it right. The heat is mouth-tingling but won’t burn the roof off, and it’s nicely balanced by the tasty spicing and fresh lime. This is not a gourmet product- the chicken for instance has no discernible meat texture. What it is is a very delicious, reasonably healthy and well-priced answer to the daily lunch dilemma. I would attach a photo but we scoffed the rolls too quick ;)

product_name: Sainsbury's Takeaway Fully Loaded Meat Feast Pizza 567g
title: OK if you like half and half
text: These are great if you only want toppings on 1 side of the pizza. First off I like these pizzas. They have a good base and crust. They don't go hard like a biscuit or chewy. Most of the time the base crisps up nicely. They also have a good amount of topping. Some people say they don't like the artificial garlic flavour but I prefer it to some of the blander tasting pizzas. Why only 2 stars? The problem is every one I have bought the topping has fallen to one side leaving the other side with no toppings. I have to rearrange the toppings before cooking. It's not because I was carrying them home upright on one side. I carried one home when the weather was -2 and the topping was still only on one side. It's only a 10 minute walk home so with the temperature the pizza didn't have a chance to defrost on the way home. It's because they are stored upright on their sides in a warm environment either while they are being transported to the store or when they are delivered to the store they are left out of the freezer waiting to be taken out to the shop floor. The last time I bought one I felt every pizza in the freezer and I could feel the bulge through the cardboard where the toppings were all on one side. See the photos. They are 3 different pizzas. Also these are quite clearly frozen pizzas because I got them out of the freezer in the frozen food area of the store. It's not rocket science. Come on Sainsbury's, sort it out.

The problem is every one I have bought the topping has fallen to one side leaving the other side with no toppings. I have to rearrange the toppings before cooking. It's not because I was carrying them home upright on one side. I carried one home when the weather was -2 and the topping was still only on one side. It's only a 10 minute walk home so with the temperature the pizza didn't have a chance to defrost on the way home. It's because they are stored upright on their sides in a warm environment either while they are being transported to the store or when they are delivered to the store they are left out of the freezer waiting to be taken out to the shop floor. The last time I bought one I felt every pizza in the freezer and I could feel the bulge through the cardboard where the toppings were all on one side. See the photos. They are 3 different pizzas. Also these are quite clearly frozen pizzas because I got them out of the freezer in the frozen food area of the store. It's not rocket science. Come on Sainsbury's, sort it out.

title: Mushy, greasy and shrink to size of a penny
text: If you like your burgers to shrink to the size of a penny and leave you with a pan full of grease and mush, you are in for treat (never seen a burger shrink so much!). They taste and look vile. And the meat content is poor as well, 72% with 14% Onion (not everyone likes onion so why add it!). Sainsburys own brand frozen equivalent has 82% meat in and no onion. And as one reveiwer has already pointed out they claim its made with 100% British or Irish Beef. This is deceptive to say the least as its clearly not 100% meat in the burger!, what they should say its made with British or Irish Beef BirdsEye really have gone downhill in recent years with quality, yet continue to rely on their big brand name, when clearly they have sneaked in cheaper ingrediants. Buy supermarket own, much nicer, more meat and better value

product_name: Twinings the Earl Grey Decaffeinated Plant Based Tea Bags x40 100g
title: Twinings Earl Grey Decaffeinated – Classic Flavour
text: If you love classic Earl Grey but want to avoid caffeine, Twinings Earl Grey Decaffeinated is a fantastic option. This caffeine-free version of one of the world’s most iconic teas still retains its signature taste and aroma. Once brewed, it has a beautiful amber colour and releases a delicate citrusy scent of bergamot. The flavour is smooth yet distinctive, with a subtle bitterness from the black tea and a refreshing bergamot finish. Compared to the regular version, it feels slightly lighter but still maintains its elegance. It tastes great on its own or with a splash of milk or a slice of lemon. Perfect for an evening wind-down when you crave something flavourful but don’t want to risk sleeplessness.

product_name: Weetabix 24 Oatibix Cereal
title: Improvement on the original Weetabix
text: An interesting twist on the original, these oat-based Weetabix are similar but quite different. The texture is not as crunchy for as long (which was never long enough in the first place), and they turn into a porridgy mush quite fast, even with cold milk. I have to add one at a time to milk and wolf it down fast to get my preferred bite. The original are woeful in filling me up - I can quite literally eat an entire stack for breakfast, which is outrageous - whereas these have a bit more substance and I feel fuller sooner. So these are an improvement in my book, but will never really challenge my preferred whole grain breakfast cereal.

text: I fully agree with all the complaints here about the carton and its opening. Awful!! Not worth the hassle. Most of the own brand juices seem to now come in these cheap, flimsy cartons that don't stand up to the pressure you need to apply to open the carton with these terrible new types of opening lids. The whole carton just bends/caves in as the lid only slightly opens a bit at a time and the juice goes everywhere as it spills out while you are trying to open the carton. I have discovered that this laundry detergent doesn't leave behind any type of residue when the clothing is both wet and once dry which is good. No white stripy marks left on our dark colours here. But the cleaning element and the removing of stains just isn't there and these are garments that are not even heavily soiled or dirty. Such a shame as I really wanted to like this product but unfortunately that is not to be the case.

title: Not as good as it used to be.
text: I have used this product since its introduction a number of years ago. Today’s product is very different. Each sheet is thinner and smaller, probably something to do with being flushable, which is obviously a good thing. My biggest problem now is the interleaving of the sheets, supposedly so that you can take one sheet at a time, however this no longer happens. They come out in a big bunch of sheets, which you then have to try to stuff back in again. Not hygienic for the next person but also the pack is difficult to re-seal which means the sheets can dry out quickly. For a premium priced product I expect better.

product_name: Sainsbury's 2 Signature Beef Rib & Briskett Burgers, Taste the Difference 340g
title: A poor burger hidden behind an excellent name
text: Bought these burgers based on the many excellent reviews. Despite them being pricey. We couldn’t have been more disappointed. On paper they sound superb, and with those ingredients they should be the tastiest burger you’ve ever tasted…but they are not. The burger is a good size. And a good texture. But that’s where the positives end. They are incredibly under-seasoned and the bone marrow makes every bite very very greasy. All you can taste is grease. No beefy flavour. Zero beef rib flavour. Zero brisket flavour. Just grease. Another example of a potentially great burger that’s not been executed well enough. A waste of money.

product_name: Sainsbury's Fruit Juice Ice Cream Splits 6x73ml
title: Good value and delicious!
text: I buy a pack of these every week. You don't always get 2 of each flavour, you might get an extra pineapple instead of a strawberry, but we're not bothered about that. We enjoy the flavours, they're just the right size, and the ratio of ice to icecream is nicely balanced. Tasty and refreshing all year round, a low calorie dessert option, and at a very good price for 6 lollies. I don't understand the reviews posted in recent months commenting that they're tasteless. We've never bought a bad box. Highly recommended!

product_name: Douwe Egberts Pure Indulgence Instant Coffee 190g
title: A Medium Strength Coffee
text: I usually like my coffee to have a bit of strength to it, but I thought I would give the Douwe Egberts Pure Indulgence instant coffee a go. The jar states that it has a strength rating of 4 (out of 5) and I would personally say that a rating of 3 would be more appropriate. I like a large mug of coffee and my usual choices of coffee takes two heaped teaspoons to get it to my preferred consistency. On this occasion, I needed three spoons of the Douwe Egberts. I may, as I have done in the past, mix a weaker coffee with my usual strong coffee to make full use of it. This product was purchased as a part of an offer.

title: They’ve ruined them with the jam change!
text: I used to really like these and bought them regularly. Recently they’ve changed the jam - new packaging which says “new fruitier jam”. The jam doesn’t taste much different but it’s tough and chewy, and even more annoyingly, it’s now only in the middle of the biscuit, it doesn’t go to the edges, so there is quite a big gap between the two biscuit layers, which then means when you bite into it the biscuit crumbles apart! I wonder if the “new fruitier jam” is just an excuse to use less jam and make it cheaper to produce! If so id rather they had kept them how they were and just put the price up instead!

title: I gave it 1 star because I could not give it 0
text: I bought this as I have had taste the difference Indian meals and enjoyed them. This however is disgusting. It is so awful there is little benefit in eating it, so I didn't. The only difference in the taste is the terrible quality in comaprison to the cook seperatley sweet and sour chicken. There were 3 bits of chicken smaller than the pineapple of which there were 2 peices. The egg fried rice was mostly lumps of egg and what rice there was looked and tasted odd. The sweet and sour sauce was so bitter it nearly covered the disgusting taste of the rest of it but not nearly enough for me to still eat it. Sainsbury's CHinese range has never been great but had improved a lot a couple of years back so I risked it. Never again. Taste the difference! the only difference being how much it cost for such terrible food. Stick to the real thing in future

product_name: Young's Salmon Pie 375g
title: So so
text: It's everything you'd expect from this type of meal. Only enough as a snack....It started off well....I thought the mash & sauce tasted nice together & didn't really need extra salt. From there it went downhill...less than halfway across the tray the sauce disappeared & was left with dry mash. Seems the sauce is dumped in at one end...just as well as there'd hardly have been any if it was spread out evenly. There was plenty of small chunks of salmon scattered through...I had to leave these as I HATE salmon! (The wife bought me this meal!). I came across two small bones...another reviewer below also mentioned this... so maybe not a meal for the younger ones. Wouldn't have again.

product_name: Birds Eye Classic Beef Quarter Pounders with Onion x4 454g
title: MISLEADING PACKAGING FOR INFERIOR PRODUCT
text: Birds Eye boast "made with 100% British and Irish beef". What they really mean is that it's not horsemeat, or beef that is some other nationality. If you don't read the small print you might assume they contain 100% beef, when the truth is they contain a pitiful 72%. The trick of being dishonest without technically lying. At least the Sainsbury and Stamford ones tell you up front what the percentage is. Birds Eye charge more for less meat, same with their fish fingers which have less fish.

product_name: Andrex® Ultimate Quilts Toilet Tissue
title: Extra Comfort
text: I’ve always bought Andrex, but the Ultimate Quilted version is definitely a step up. It’s noticeably thicker, softer, and more cushioned, which makes it feel much more premium and comfortable to use. You can tell the difference straight away, it feels stronger but still really gentle on the skin. It doesn't tear easily, and you end up needing less because it’s more absorbent. It’s definitely more expensive than the standard Andrex, but for the added comfort, thickness and quality, I’d say it’s worth it.

product_name: Mackie's of Scotland Luxury Traditional Real Dairy Ice Cream 1L
title: Nowhere near as good as it used to be
text: We've had this many times over the years, and it's been really nice. Unfortunately, the last tubs have been pretty poor. It still tastes reasonable enough, but the texture is terrible. It used to be proper thick stuff, a bit hard to get out of the tub. However, now it's mushy semi-liquid - something like the texture of double cream, not ice cream. We thought our freezer must have failed when we got it out, but no, it's this ice cream. It's sold by volume, not weight. We weighed it - at something like 550g for a litre, it's not much more than half the density of water - it must contain a lot of air. Sadly, we'd bought six tubs based on how it used to be: we'll go elsewhere next time.

product_name: Sainsbury's Thai Red Prawn Curry with Jasmine Rice Ready Meal For 1, Taste the Difference 400g
title: Superb - a treat
text: This was delivered with plenty of use by date and remained fresh in the fridge whilst waiting to use. Quick and easy to microwave, just a quick stir and some water added to rice half way through. 6 prawns, not massive but tasty. 1 small piece of babycorn, more crunchy bamboo shoot and vibrant green spring greens. Some soft red pepper slices. The rice is sticky and fragrant, excellent in combination with the Thai red sauce. Just the right level of heat. The sauce contains lots of real dairy cream, so the meal is rich and satisfying (and a bit greasy!). Portion size fine. Left me feeling treated, satisfied and wanting this again another time.

Initially thought it might have been a one off jar or something, purchased another and the very same very bland taste from that one also. I realise others have been told all that has changed is the label, but I refuse to agree with that. IMO something else has changed as the aroma and the taste is now completely different and very bland. Will not be purchasing anymore Alta Rica until I hear somewhere that it has reverted back to the taste it was.

product_name: Weetos 420g Chocolatey Hoops Breakfast Cereal
title: Nostalgic childhood favourite
text: Weetos Chocolatey Hoops are a nostalgic childhood favourite that I occasionally indulge in even as an adult. These crunchy, chocolatey cereal hoops take me back to those mornings when breakfast was a mini celebration.What I appreciate most about Weetos is the balance between chocolate flavour and cereal crunch. The hoops are generously coated with a rich chocolatey layer, providing that satisfying sweetness without being overwhelming. They hold up well in milk, maintaining their crunchiness and chocolatey goodness throughout breakfast.As a parent, I've found that Weetos can also be a fun way to get kids excited about breakfast. The unique hoop shape and chocolatey taste make it appealing to children, and it's a cereal they genuinely enjoy eating.The packaging is practical, keeping the cereal fresh and easy to pour. However, as with many sugary cereals, it's essential to consume Weetos in moderation due to their sugar content.In summary, Weetos Chocolatey Hoops are a tasty breakfast option that evokes fond memories of childhood mornings. They strike a delightful balance between chocolate flavour and cereal crunch, making them a treat for all ages. Just remember to enjoy them as part of a balanced diet.

product_name: Mackie's of Scotland Raspberry Ripple Real Dairy Ice Cream 1L

text: I honestly don't know how this ice-cream has had any positive reviews. The ripple is far too sweet and is concentrated just at the top and bottom. Not the ripple consistency I remember, it is basically a sugary sauce bunged on the ice-cream. The ice-cream itself bears no resemblance to clotted cream, it has very little flavour and an unpleasant texture. I would not buy this product again, or even eat it if it was free.
full_text: Awful Ice-cream. I honestly don't know how this ice-cream has had any positive reviews. The ripple is far too sweet and is concentrated just at the top and bottom. product_name: NESCAFÉ Cappuccino
title: Disappointing for a Coffee Lover
text: As a big coffee drinker, I was really hoping Nescafé’s Instant Cappuccino would be a convenient and tasty option for busy mornings. On the positive side, the packaging was clear, the instructions were easy to follow, and making the drink couldn’t have been simpler. I also appreciated the nice layer of foam on top—it looked promising. Unfortunately, the taste just didn’t live up to expectations. It had a very artificial flavor that didn’t remind me of a real cappuccino at all. I’d planned to keep these in my work bag as a quick grab-and-go option to save some money on coffee runs, but it just wasn’t good enough to make the switch. If you’re not too fussy about coffee flavor, it might work for you

Whether it’s mixing them in a spaghetti bolognaise, putting them on side of a pie or enjoying them with tasty fish fingers; these petit pois are a tasty and healthy accompaniment to any meal! Make sure you’ve always go a pack ready in your freezer; we certainly do!

title: Perfect crunch
text: As a fan of TTD products, I was not disappointed when I first tried these crisps. I buy them nearly every week. At my age (75) I have to ration treats for health reasons so it's important to have the best. Apart from the taste, it's the crunch that counts. it bridges the gap between a standard, thin crisp and the deliberately mega-thick varieties. I may be misusing the term, but for me it is the "mouth feel". Usually applied to things that are soft or chewy, it is the moment of perfect crunch and munch.

product_name: Kellogg's All-Bran Breakfast Cereal 750g
title: New Recipe has ruined the taste!
text: I have enjoyed Kellogs All Bran for over 50 years up until a few weeks ago when the recipe changed. It has been ruined! It is tasteless and a bit like eating cardboard. I used to mix it with Frosties which was a delicious combination. The new formula is such a disappointment and the bran does not soften with the milk. I will not be buying it again but will have to look at own store products to find the nearest alternative which closest resembles the original formula. The so called panel of 'experts' who approved this change should hang their heads in shame. I think that falling sales will be the proof of this marketing folly!

title: So tasty …but carrageenan strikes again!
text: I was so happy to finally be able to enjoy ice cream again, without the worry of dairy intolerance repercussions. However, I was confused why I then experienced the usual telltale bloating and uncomfortable stomach. Having looked further, the stabilising ingredient carrageenan, is responsible for causing problem in people suffering with ibs type issues. I have also found it in coconut collab and plant based cheese. It’s such a shame. Manufacturers please rethink and consider those with limited options, would be delighted if you could.

product_name: Sainsbury's 30 Days Matured British Beef Picanha Steak, Taste the Difference 225g
title: Favourite cut, terrible supplier
text: I love picanha. When Sainsbury's first introduced it I was hooked and it was perfect every time. However, over the last couple of years it has been terrible for several reasons. Firstly, availability. Too often it just won't be available in my local store. No obvious reason and no shortage of other cuts. Secondly, occasionally (and more often than I would like) it is just of poor quality. Tough, grisly and tasteless. But by far the biggest problem is the "butchery" by the supplier. Cut much too thinly or wedged so there is no way to cook it evenly, missing the strip of fat that is expected of this cut, simply messy with straggly bits and pieces tucked away underneath to hide them, and the list goes on. One of the biggest mistakes made by Sainsbury's is getting rid of the butcher counter, where you could get your steak cut exactly to your liking. If this picanha steak were to return to the same quality as when it was first introduced to the Sainsbury's stores, then it would be an easy 5 stars. No question. However, as it is it just makes me angry every time I try to pick out a bit of steak. Even if I resort to having to choose a different cut, I still have to go through the entire contents of the fridge to try and find pieces that have not been ruined by awful butchery. Unfortunately, none of the other supermarkets seem to stock picanha, so I am currently in the process of trying to find somewhere else that I can buy it from regularly.

No obvious reason and no shortage of other cuts. Secondly, occasionally (and more often than I would like) it is just of poor quality. Tough, grisly and tasteless. But by far the biggest problem is the "butchery" by the supplier. Cut much too thinly or wedged so there is no way to cook it evenly, missing the strip of fat that is expected of this cut, simply messy with straggly bits and pieces tucked away underneath to hide them, and the list goes on. One of the biggest mistakes made by Sainsbury's is getting rid of the butcher counter, where you could get your steak cut exactly to your liking. If this picanha steak were to return to the same quality as when it was first introduced to the Sainsbury's stores, then it would be an easy 5 stars. No question. However, as it is it just makes me angry every time I try to pick out a bit of steak. Even if I resort to having to choose a different cut, I still have to go through the entire contents of the fridge to try and find pieces that have not been ruined by awful butchery. Unfortunately, none of the other supermarkets seem to stock picanha, so I am currently in the process of trying to find somewhere else that I can buy it from regularly.

It's the first time I'd bought it and I would not buy it again.

title: Quite weak tasting coffee but smooth

The packet is a refillable bag that can be re-used and re-filled once the contents have finished which is great so it saves it from being put into the bin when it simply can be re-used again and again. The contents of the granules are quite distinctive and lightly coloured caramel brown which again is very distinctive and different to my normal cup of coffee I use. I would probably required two spoonfuls of this coffee to substitute what I normally drink.

product_name: Taylors of Harrogate Rich Italian Roast Ground Coffee 200g
title: Not sure about this one
text: The words "Rich Italian Roast" attracted my eye on this one. But that needs qualifying - this is an English attempt at making ground coffee as the Italians make it. It's not a bad imitation, if not on the same level as the genuine article. The rating - 4 - is right in the middle, a rating of 7 being the most intense. Yet it feels stronger, The caffeine-and-bitterness hit is like a punch in the face, without the full-bodiedness that would carry it. I’ve even considered adding a dash of cocoa powder to round out the flavour profile. (Haven’t tried that yet.) So, I’m not sure of this one. I intend to try various other coffees before returning to this one, if I ever do. I’m starting to wonder if a coffee with a 3 rating, but with a more well-rounded flavour profile, might be more to my taste.

text: So good to have access to fresh herbs, with all the advantages of freshly-frozen. The packaging is a total let-down. What food-ignorant person came up with this design, and why sold here? Picard in France do a superb iteration of this idea, and it's so easy to store and easy too to prevent clumping (which is what frozen products do when they're exposed, even momentarily, to room-temperature air) by giving a quick shake before re-storing. No-brainer

product_name: Sainsbury's Just Cook Butter Basted Chicken Breast Joint 350g
title: Substitute for garlic crown for Christmas Day!
text: Ordered the garlic chicken crown to arrive on Christmas Eve delivery for the two meat eaters in the family, but it was out of stock so it was substituted with this chicken joint. This chicken joint was smaller than the chicken crown I'd ordered, which wasn't ideal as it wasn't really large enough for two decent portions. I had the choice of accepting it or going out to the shops to try and find something else for the meat eaters, as we live in a rural area and it's quite a journey to the shops, along with the fact that we already had a houseful of guests that I would have had to have left to go to the shops meant that I opted to keep it. There was a lot of pink liquid in the bottom of the tray, and the joint shrunk a lot during cooking, there was also a pink, bloody area on the base of the joint that stayed pink and bloody looking even after thorough cooking, which looked very unpleasant so I cut that bit off. There wasn't really a good portion size for each of the two meat eaters because it shrunk so much, but I'd done lots of veggie dishes, so they didn't go hungry!! Probably wouldn't buy again.

product_name: Sainsbury's Petits Pois 910g
title: tasted more like tiny garden peas
text: Y'know, I generally love petits pots. I can literally eat a whole bowlful of them for my tea. I usually buy Birds Eye from my local corner shop - but on this occasion I went for the Sainsbury's option. Viewer, they were not good. They were not sweet like petit pots usually are. They tasted like normal peas! Like the sorts of vegetables we ate in the 70s and 80s. I couldn't eat more than a small portion of them, tbh. V bland and not enticing to this person's palate at all. I will probably let the rest of the pack thaw out and then use them to feed the ducks - a top tip, there, as peas are full of protein and much better for them than your old stale bread.

product_name: Sainsbury's 1.9kg Stock Basted Whole Chicken
title: This is Good Chicken
text: This is my go-to chicken in a bag from Sainsbury's. The lemon is very subtle and there are loads of juices to use in a gravy - if you know what you're doing. After resting and carving up the breast meat I usually put aside the legs, wings and darker meat for use in other meals such as curries, pizzas or a simple chicken and gravy pie. You should have left over meat when serving 4 adults if you have a decent portion of veg. Those reviewers throwing their chickens in the bin should stick to the ready meal isle or continue to eat bland chicken covered in hot sauce. Only complaint is that occasionally the packers have placed the chicken upside down which has led to the juices being lost from the tray.

product_name: Kellogg's Rice Krispies Breakfast Cereal 430g
title: Love Rice Krispies!
text: Kellogg’s Rice Krispies are one of those comforting staples I always come back to. Light, crisp, and not overly sweet, they’re perfect when you want something simple that just works. The texture stays satisfying without feeling heavy, and that classic crackle never gets old. They’re great on their own with cold milk, but just as good dressed up with fruit, honey, or even a spoonful of sugar when you’re feeling nostalgic. I also love how versatile they are — from quick breakfasts to baking (Rice Krispie treats will always be elite). A proper cupboard essential, especially for days when you want something gentle, familiar, and reliable. Sometimes the classics really don’t need improving.

product_name: Kenco Cappuccino Unsweetened Instant Coffee 8 Sachets
title: Unfortunately spoiled by too weak coffee flavour
text: A pleasant hot creamy, frothy, milk beverage with relatively low sugar but certainly far too weak coffee flavour. The Nescafe equivalent has, in our opinion, about the right strength of flavour but is about 50% more expensive at full price levels. Why no own brand equivalent anywhere ? - Morrisons used to do one but has not done so since shortly after the recent change of ownership. This Kenco version is a pale imitation of the market leader and should be reformulated. Ok if you add a half teaspoonful of a good 'gold' instant coffee, but that should already be in the contents. In our opinion ruined by the current formulation, which could so easily be changed to rival that same market leader.

In our opinion ruined by the current formulation, which could so easily be changed to rival that same market leader.

I am now purchasing the sunday joint elsewhere.
